skill.name: angular-best-practices
skill.description: Angular performance optimization guide for writing, reviewing, and refactoring Angular applications with efficient rendering, bundles, SSR, and state patterns.
skill.summary: Apply Angular performance best practices for change detection, lazy loading, rendering, SSR, and memory cleanup.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are false positives from Markdown formatting, documentation URLs, and Angular TypeScript examples. No executable skill code, prompt injection, credential access, command execution, or data exfiltration behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Static Shell Execution Matches Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick patterns are Markdown inline code and fenced Angular examples. They do not execute commands or instruct the agent to run shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are documentation prose, Markdown code spans, or TypeScript code fences. No executable script file or command invocation path exists in the skill.

low_findings.title: Network Matches Are Documentation References and Local Examples
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs point to Angular and web.dev documentation. The HTTP get examples are Angular application snippets using local API paths, not outbound skill behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are reference links, and the HTTP calls are inside illustrative Angular snippets. I found no code that sends user data or secrets to an external endpoint.

low_findings.title: Dynamic Import Matches Are Angular Lazy Loading Guidance
low_findings.description: Dynamic import expressions appear in examples for route lazy loading and on-demand library loading. They are not used by the skill to load arbitrary code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The imports are static string module paths inside Angular performance examples. There is no user-controlled import source or executable package logic.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Matches Are Non-Security Prose or Angular Code
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations contain performance descriptions, Angular examples, or ordinary string filtering logic. No hash, cipher, or cryptographic API usage was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of representative high-severity lines found no cryptographic operations. The detector appears to have matched unrelated text or code tokens.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Matches Are Angular Section Text
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance findings point to Angular headings and component examples. They do not query host details, enumerate files, or inspect the runtime environment.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation for SSR, barrel imports, deferred components, and templates. No reconnaissance command or host inspection behavior is present.

content.value_statement: Angular applications often lose speed through inefficient change detection, large bundles, and avoidable render work. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured checklist for performance-focused Angular decisions.
actual_capabilities: Reviews Angular components for OnPush change detection, Signals, and efficient state updates.
Identifies async waterfall patterns and recommends parallel or flattened data fetching.
Guides lazy loading, deferred views, and direct imports to reduce bundle size.
Checks list rendering, virtual scrolling, pure pipes, and computed values for render efficiency.
Provides SSR, hydration, TransferState, and memory cleanup guidance for Angular applications.
limitations: It is a guidance skill and does not run benchmarks or measure real browser performance.
It does not replace Angular documentation for version-specific API changes.
It may need project context to choose between Signals, RxJS, NgRx, or existing patterns.
It does not automatically modify code unless the connected AI tool performs edits.

use_cases.title: Review a New Angular Feature
use_cases.description: Check a new component or route for change detection, lazy loading, list rendering, and subscription cleanup before merge.

use_cases.title: Plan a Performance Refactor
use_cases.description: Turn broad performance concerns into a prioritized backlog for bundles, rendering, data fetching, and SSR improvements.

use_cases.title: Teach Angular Performance Patterns
use_cases.description: Explain modern Angular practices with clear correct and incorrect examples for a team learning Signals and hydration.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Component Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Angular component for performance basics. Focus on change detection, Signals, template expressions, and subscription cleanup.

prompt_templates.title: Route and Bundle Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Angular feature for bundle size and loading behavior. Check lazy routes, deferred components, direct imports, and heavy dependencies.

prompt_templates.title: SSR and Hydration Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this Angular page for SSR and hydration performance. Identify client-side waterfalls, missing TransferState usage, and non-critical hydration work.

prompt_templates.title: Full Performance Refactor Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a prioritized Angular performance refactor plan for this project. Group findings by impact, explain tradeoffs, and list safe incremental changes.

output_examples.input: Review a product list component that renders hundreds of items and filters in the template.
output_examples.output: A prioritized review that recommends stable item tracking, virtual scrolling for large lists, and moving filtering into a computed value or pure pipe.

output_examples.input: Assess an Angular admin route that imports charts and tables in the main bundle.
output_examples.output: A bundle-focused assessment that suggests lazy route loading, deferred chart rendering, and direct imports for shared UI components.

output_examples.input: Check a server-rendered profile page that fetches data after the component starts.
output_examples.output: An SSR review that flags the client-side waterfall and recommends resolver-based preload or TransferState-backed data reuse.

best_practices: Provide the Angular version, component code, route configuration, and relevant service code when asking for a review.
Ask for prioritized findings so the output separates critical performance issues from optional cleanup.
Validate recommendations with project measurements such as bundle reports, browser traces, or Angular DevTools.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for generic Angular advice without sharing the component or route context.
Do not apply every recommendation mechanically when it conflicts with established project architecture.
Do not treat documentation examples as a substitute for measuring production performance.

faq.question: Does this skill edit Angular code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides review guidance and refactor recommendations. Code edits depend on the AI tool using the skill.

faq.question: Which Angular topics does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers OnPush, Signals, zoneless change detection, async patterns, lazy loading, rendering, SSR, hydration, and cleanup.

faq.question: Can it help with older Angular projects?
faq.answer: Yes, but some recommendations depend on Angular version. Share the project version before asking for changes.

faq.question: Is it useful for NgRx applications?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes selector and state subscription guidance, but it still needs project-specific store context.

faq.question: Does it measure performance?
faq.answer: No. It identifies likely performance issues and suggests checks. Use browser tooling and bundle analysis for measurement.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed files contain documentation and Angular examples only, with no confirmed malicious behavior.
